## Your Task

Review the bug fixes from Phase 08. Verify each fix addresses the
reported bug without introducing regressions or scope expansion.
Then commit the fixes.

## Mode: {MODE}

**If mode is `iteration`:** Focus your review on the CHANGES made this
round. Don't re-review unchanged code unless a change introduces a
regression risk in adjacent code.

## Style Inspiration: {STYLE_NAME}

{STYLE_PRINCIPLES}

## Review Process

### Pass 1: Fix Verification

Read the fix log at `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-08-implement-fix.md`.
For each fix:
- [ ] Fix addresses the root cause (not just the symptom)
- [ ] Fix is minimal (no unnecessary changes)
- [ ] A regression test exists that reproduces the original bug
- [ ] No new security issues introduced
- [ ] No scope expansion (feature addition, refactoring)

If any fix fails these checks: fix it directly.

### Pass 2: Mechanical Issues (Fix-First)

Scan the modified files for:
- Dead code introduced by the fix — [AUTO-FIXED] remove it
- Stale comments contradicting the fix — [AUTO-FIXED] update them
- Missing error handling around the fix — [AUTO-FIXED] add it

For each mechanical fix: apply it and log as [AUTO-FIXED].

### Pass 3: Commit

1. Run all tests. Fix any regressions.
2. Clean up debug artifacts.
3. Stage and commit:
   ```bash
   git add output/
   git commit -m "fix: [one-line description of bugs fixed]"
   ```
4. Verify clean working tree.

## Output Format

Write to `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-09-review-and-commit-fix.md`:

```markdown
# Bug Fix Review & Commit — Run {RUN_ID}

## Fix Verification
1. [bug]: VERIFIED / NEEDS REWORK — [notes]

## Auto-Fixed Issues
- [AUTO-FIXED] [file:line] — [what was done]

## Scope Check
- New features added: 0 (must be 0)
- Files modified outside bug scope: [list or none]

## Commit
- Hash: [short hash]
- Message: [commit message]

## Test Results
[paste test output]
```
